  scope: Construct,

##### `delete_restored_instance`<sup>Optional</sup> <a name="delete_restored_instance" id="@cdktn/provider-google-beta.googleBackupDrRestoreWorkload.GoogleBackupDrRestoreWorkload.Initializer.parameter.deleteRestoredInstance"></a>

- *Type:* bool | cdktn.IResolvable

Optional.

If true (default), running terraform destroy will delete the live resource in GCP.
If false, only the restore record is removed from the state, leaving the resource active.

Docs at Terraform Registry: {@link https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/google-beta/7.34.0/docs/resources/google_backup_dr_restore_workload#delete_restored_instance GoogleBackupDrRestoreWorkload#delete_restored_instance}

---

##### `deletion_policy`<sup>Optional</sup> <a name="deletion_policy" id="@cdktn/provider-google-beta.googleBackupDrRestoreWorkload.GoogleBackupDrRestoreWorkload.Initializer.parameter.deletionPolicy"></a>

- *Type:* str

Whether Terraform will be prevented from destroying the instance.

Defaults to "DELETE".
When a 'terraform destroy' or 'terraform apply' would delete the instance,
the command will fail if this field is set to "PREVENT" in Terraform state.
When set to "ABANDON", the command will remove the resource from Terraform
management without updating or deleting the resource in the API.
When set to "DELETE", deleting the resource is allowed.

Docs at Terraform Registry: {@link https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/google-beta/7.34.0/docs/resources/google_backup_dr_restore_workload#deletion_policy GoogleBackupDrRestoreWorkload#deletion_policy}

---

##### `with` <a name="with" id="@cdktn/provider-google-beta.googleBackupDrRestoreWorkload.GoogleBackupDrRestoreWorkload.with"></a>

```python
def with(
  mixins: *IMixin
) -> IConstruct
```

Applies one or more mixins to this construct.

Mixins are applied in order. The list of constructs is captured at the
start of the call, so constructs added by a mixin will not be visited.
Use multiple `with()` calls if subsequent mixins should apply to added
constructs.

###### `mixins`<sup>Required</sup> <a name="mixins" id="@cdktn/provider-google-beta.googleBackupDrRestoreWorkload.GoogleBackupDrRestoreWorkload.with.parameter.mixins"></a>

- *Type:* *constructs.IMixin

The mixins to apply.

```python
def put_disk_restore_properties(
  name: str,

  access_mode: str = None,
  architecture: str = None,
  description: str = None,
  disk_encryption_key: GoogleBackupDrRestoreWorkloadDiskRestorePropertiesDiskEncryptionKey = None,
  enable_confidential_compute: bool | IResolvable = None,
  guest_os_feature: IResolvable | typing.List[GoogleBackupDrRestoreWorkloadDiskRestorePropertiesGuestOsFeature] = None,
  labels: IResolvable | typing.List[GoogleBackupDrRestoreWorkloadDiskRestorePropertiesLabels] = None,
  licenses: typing.List[str] = None,
  physical_block_size_bytes: typing.Union[int, float] = None,
  provisioned_iops: typing.Union[int, float] = None,
  provisioned_throughput: typing.Union[int, float] = None,
  resource_manager_tags: IResolvable | typing.List[GoogleBackupDrRestoreWorkloadDiskRestorePropertiesResourceManagerTags] = None,
  resource_policy: typing.List[str] = None,
  storage_pool: str = None
) -> None
```

##### `is_construct` <a name="is_construct" id="@cdktn/provider-google-beta.googleBackupDrRestoreWorkload.GoogleBackupDrRestoreWorkload.isConstruct"></a>

```python
from cdktn_provider_google_beta import google_backup_dr_restore_workload

googleBackupDrRestoreWorkload.GoogleBackupDrRestoreWorkload.is_construct(
  x: typing.Any
)
```

Checks if `x` is a construct.

Use this method instead of `instanceof` to properly detect `Construct`
instances, even when the construct library is symlinked.

Explanation: in JavaScript, multiple copies of the `constructs` library on
disk are seen as independent, completely different libraries. As a
consequence, the class `Construct` in each copy of the `constructs` library
is seen as a different class, and an instance of one class will not test as
`instanceof` the other class. `npm install` will not create installations
like this, but users may manually symlink construct libraries together or
use a monorepo tool: in those cases, multiple copies of the `constructs`
library can be accidentally installed, and `instanceof` will behave
unpredictably. It is safest to avoid using `instanceof`, and using
this type-testing method instead.

###### `x`<sup>Required</sup> <a name="x" id="@cdktn/provider-google-beta.googleBackupDrRestoreWorkload.GoogleBackupDrRestoreWorkload.isConstruct.parameter.x"></a>

- *Type:* typing.Any

Any object.

---

  scope: Construct,

  import_from_id: str,

)

### GoogleBackupDrRestoreWorkloadComputeInstanceRestorePropertiesAdvancedMachineFeatures <a name="GoogleBackupDrRestoreWorkloadComputeInstanceRestorePropertiesAdvancedMachineFeatures" id="@cdktn/provider-google-beta.googleBackupDrRestoreWorkload.GoogleBackupDrRestoreWorkloadComputeInstanceRestorePropertiesAdvancedMachineFeatures"></a>

#### Initializer <a name="Initializer" id="@cdktn/provider-google-beta.googleBackupDrRestoreWorkload.GoogleBackupDrRestoreWorkloadComputeInstanceRestorePropertiesAdvancedMachineFeatures.Initializer"></a>

```python
from cdktn_provider_google_beta import google_backup_dr_restore_workload

googleBackupDrRestoreWorkload.GoogleBackupDrRestoreWorkloadComputeInstanceRestorePropertiesAdvancedMachineFeatures(
  enable_nested_virtualization: bool | IResolvable = None,
  enable_uefi_networking: bool | IResolvable = None,
  threads_per_core: typing.Union[int, float] = None,
  visible_core_count: typing.Union[int, float] = None
)
```

#### Properties <a name="Properties" id="Properties"></a>

| **Name** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| <code><a href="#@cdktn/provider-google-beta.googleBackupDrRestoreWorkload.GoogleBackupDrRestoreWorkloadComputeInstanceRestorePropertiesAdvancedMachineFeatures.property.enableNestedVirtualization">enable_nested_virtualization</a></code> | <code>bool \| cdktn.IResolvable</code> | Optional. Whether to enable nested virtualization or not (default is false). |
| <code><a href="#@cdktn/provider-google-beta.googleBackupDrRestoreWorkload.GoogleBackupDrRestoreWorkloadComputeInstanceRestorePropertiesAdvancedMachineFeatures.property.enableUefiNetworking">enable_uefi_networking</a></code> | <code>bool \| cdktn.IResolvable</code> | Optional. Whether to enable UEFI networking for instance creation. |
| <code><a href="#@cdktn/provider-google-beta.googleBackupDrRestoreWorkload.GoogleBackupDrRestoreWorkloadComputeInstanceRestorePropertiesAdvancedMachineFeatures.property.threadsPerCore">threads_per_core</a></code> | <code>typing.Union[int, float]</code> | Optional. The number of threads per physical core. |
| <code><a href="#@cdktn/provider-google-beta.googleBackupDrRestoreWorkload.GoogleBackupDrRestoreWorkloadComputeInstanceRestorePropertiesAdvancedMachineFeatures.property.visibleCoreCount">visible_core_count</a></code> | <code>typing.Union[int, float]</code> | Optional. The number of physical cores to expose to an instance. |

##### `delete_restored_instance`<sup>Optional</sup> <a name="delete_restored_instance" id="@cdktn/provider-google-beta.googleBackupDrRestoreWorkload.GoogleBackupDrRestoreWorkloadConfig.property.deleteRestoredInstance"></a>

```python
delete_restored_instance: bool | IResolvable
```

- *Type:* bool | cdktn.IResolvable

Optional.

If true (default), running terraform destroy will delete the live resource in GCP.
If false, only the restore record is removed from the state, leaving the resource active.

Docs at Terraform Registry: {@link https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/google-beta/7.34.0/docs/resources/google_backup_dr_restore_workload#delete_restored_instance GoogleBackupDrRestoreWorkload#delete_restored_instance}

---

##### `deletion_policy`<sup>Optional</sup> <a name="deletion_policy" id="@cdktn/provider-google-beta.googleBackupDrRestoreWorkload.GoogleBackupDrRestoreWorkloadConfig.property.deletionPolicy"></a>

```python
deletion_policy: str
```

- *Type:* str

Whether Terraform will be prevented from destroying the instance.

Defaults to "DELETE".
When a 'terraform destroy' or 'terraform apply' would delete the instance,
the command will fail if this field is set to "PREVENT" in Terraform state.
When set to "ABANDON", the command will remove the resource from Terraform
management without updating or deleting the resource in the API.
When set to "DELETE", deleting the resource is allowed.

Docs at Terraform Registry: {@link https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/google-beta/7.34.0/docs/resources/google_backup_dr_restore_workload#deletion_policy GoogleBackupDrRestoreWorkload#deletion_policy}

---

from cdktn_provider_google_beta import google_backup_dr_restore_workload

googleBackupDrRestoreWorkload.GoogleBackupDrRestoreWorkloadDiskRestoreProperties(
  name: str,

  type: str,
  access_mode: str = None,
  architecture: str = None,

```python
creation_stack: typing.List[str]
```

- *Type:* typing.List[str]

The creation stack of this resolvable which will be appended to errors thrown during resolution.

If this returns an empty array the stack will not be attached.
